title: Figure 4 from: Yurina V, Freddy J, Aryo Wicaksono S, Rahayu Adianingsih O, Triastuti E, Shimosato T (2026) Effect of growth temperature, time, and storage on plasmid stability in recombinant Lactococcus lactis expressing SARS-CoV-2 HCR spike antigen. Pharmacia 73: e185770. https://doi.org/10.3897/pharmacia.73.e185770
author: Yurina, Valentina
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 4 Result of electrophoresis of L. lactis colonies. A. Incubation temperature 25 °C; B. Incubation temperature 30 °C. The top gel (1–14) is the PCR product amplified using the HCR-CTB primer, and the lower gel (15–28) is the PCR product amplified using the HCR primer. 1: pNZ8149-24 h, 2: pNZ8149-48 h, 3: pNZ8149-72 h, 4–6: pNZ8149-HCR-CTB-24 h, 7–9: pNZ8149-HCR-CTB-48 h, 10–12: pNZ8149-HCR-CTB-72 h, 13(A) and 14(B): pNZ8149-HCR-CTB isolate, 14(A) and 13(B): negative control (H2O), 15: pNZ8149-24 h, 16: pNZ8149-48 h, 17: pNZ8149-72 h, 18–20: pNZ8149-HCR-24 h, 21–23: pNZ8149-HCR-48 h, 24–26: pNZ8149-HCR-72 h, 27(A) and 28(B): pNZ8149-HCR isolate, 27(B) and 28(A): negative control (H2O).
steps_raw: Figure 4 Result of electrophoresis of L. lactis colonies. A. Incubation temperature 25 °C; B. Incubation temperature 30 °C.
